title: My plan on how to make Bike Schoolers money

enrichment.summary: Kyle pitches 'Bike School,' an educational system where people ride bikes 12 hours a day while listening to history content, and proposes funding it through direct personal sponsorship from billionaires — starting with Donald Trump — who would pay individual bike schoolers $50,000/year in exchange for wearing the patron's name on their jersey. He argues this model of wealth redistribution via personal patronage is preferable to taxation and draws parallels to ancient Roman clientage, the New Deal, and the king's jester relationship.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: the ideal education system — combine 12 hours of cycling with audio history learning, quantified by a timed timeline test
theoretically correct but politically unworkable because the wealthy control politicians
the best practical alternative — direct, visible, and avoids political corruption
the single biggest problem; wealth hoarding causes stagnation analogous to conditions before the Great Depression
most are soulless and evil, but their personal money can be redirected to productive use through social pressure and direct patronage
plans to wear 'Trump's Man' on his jersey for the duration of Trump's presidency whether or not Trump actually pays him
ideal — personally eats only rice, vegetables, oatmeal, protein powder, and canned fruit; bike schoolers should live like Roman soldiers
a model for a unified, brilliant, peaceful world society; its fall was caused by wealth inequality and barbarian dilution
should densify with small apartment buildings, convert CU buildings to housing, and expand bike trails rather than mountain development
dangerous; already happening with cabinet appointees; bike school patronage redirects billionaire influence away from politics
